metadata.description: Short project identifier used as a prefix for derived names. Must match the base stack.
Environment suffix (e.g. dev / prod). Must match the base stack.
Azure region for the workload. Must match the base stack; default westus3 supports Foundry Agent Service private networking.

Resource group where the BASE stack lives (VNet + subnets + private DNS zones). Defaults to `rg-ai-foundry-network-dev-westus3` per the CAF landing-zone pattern: base networking in a dedicated platform RG, workload data-plane services in a separate per-workload RG. Set to the same value as the workload RG (passed via `-g`) for the single-RG topology; override to any other name to point at a base stack owned by a different central team.
VNet name. Blank = convention.
Cognitive-PE subnet name. Blank = convention.
Storage-PE subnet name. Blank = convention.
Cosmos-PE subnet name. Blank = convention.
Search-PE subnet name. Blank = convention.
Agent (delegated) subnet name. Blank = convention.

Custom subdomain for the Foundry / Cognitive AIServices account. This is NOT the account resource name -- the account is always ais-<baseName>-<environment>-<location>. Blank = convention (cog-acc-...).

The 3 private DNS zones the Foundry account's `account` sub-resource resolves through. If you override, you must supply EXACTLY 3 zone names in the same order (cognitiveservices, openai, services.ai).

The 6 private DNS zones the Storage account's private endpoints resolve through (one per subresource, in order: blob/file/queue/table/dfs/web). If you override, you must supply EXACTLY 6 zone names in that order.
Cosmos SQL DNS zone. Default: privatelink.documents.azure.com.
AI Search DNS zone. Default: privatelink.search.windows.net.

Master switch for the public endpoint on every workload data-plane service (Storage, Cosmos, AI Search, Foundry). When true (default) the services are reachable via their public FQDN, filtered by the IP allowlist (deployerIp + allowedIpsExtra). When false the public endpoint is disabled entirely on all 4 services -- only VNet-injected agent runtime traffic can reach them via private endpoints. Flip to false to harden the deployment once you're done making changes; flip back to true before your next apply that touches Cosmos SQL role assignments or Foundry capability hosts (both of which use the data plane).

Public IPv4 of the machine running the deployment. Bare IPv4 or CIDR /0-/30. Do NOT pass /31 or /32 -- Cognitive Services rejects them; use the bare IP. Pass "" (empty) to skip adding the deployer IP -- required for the private-path deploy (deployer is on a VPN / ExpressRoute / Bastion / VNet-injected runner and reaches the workload FQDNs via the base stack's private DNS zones), and also used for the hardening step and CI runs that shouldn't advertise their runner IP. See the ai-foundry README's "Deployment topology" section for the full lifecycle.
Additional IPv4 or CIDR entries allowlisted on every workload service (teammates, office ranges, CI runner). Same format rules as deployerIp.
Tags applied to every workload resource.
Short project identifier (e.g. "ai-foundry").
Environment suffix (e.g. "dev").
Azure location.
Optional suffix appended to the derived storage account name (for uniqueness in shared subs).
Storage account SKU tier.
Storage account replication type.
Minimum TLS version.
Enable public network access. When true, the network ACL default action + allowedIps control who can reach the public endpoint.
Default action for the network rules (Deny + allowedIps = IP-allowlist mode).
IPv4 addresses / CIDR ranges allowed to reach the public endpoint when publicNetworkAccessEnabled=true.
Subnet ID where the PEs are provisioned.
Private DNS zone IDs (one list per subresource). Empty list disables that subresource's PE.
Tags applied to the storage account and its PEs.

Full storage account name override. When set (non-empty) this wins over the derived `st<baseName><environment><location><suffix>` convention. Use when the derived name would exceed 24 chars or when you need a specific globally-unique name (e.g. the terraform-state storage account uses a hashed short name). Empty string (default) triggers the derived-name path.
Private endpoint name.
Azure location.
Resource ID of the target service (e.g. storage account, cosmos, cognitive account).
List of subresource / groupId names to connect (e.g. ["blob"], ["account"], ["Sql"]).
Resource ID of the subnet where the PE NIC lives.
List of private DNS zone resource IDs that the PE registers its A record(s) in.
Optional. Custom name for the DNS zone group. Defaults to `default`.
Tags applied to the PE.

Short project identifier.
Environment suffix.
Azure location.
Cosmos DB API kind. Only SQL/NoSQL is exercised by this module today; MongoDB and other APIs need different subresource names and connection strings.
Enable public network access. Combine with ipRangeFilter for the deployer-IP allowlist pattern.
IPv4 addresses / CIDR ranges allowed on the public endpoint. Ignored when publicNetworkAccessEnabled=false.
Whether local (key) auth is enabled. Defaults to false (Entra ID / MI only). SQL API only.
Enable automatic failover.
Enable the free tier (one free-tier account per subscription).
Consistency level.
Whether the primary geo replica is zone-redundant.
Subnet ID where the PE lives.
Private DNS zone IDs for the SQL PE.
PE subresource group ID. SQL API uses "Sql".
Tags applied to the account and PE.
Private endpoint name.
Azure location.
Resource ID of the target service (e.g. storage account, cosmos, cognitive account).
List of subresource / groupId names to connect (e.g. ["blob"], ["account"], ["Sql"]).

Optional. Custom name for the DNS zone group. Defaults to `default`.
Tags applied to the PE.
Short project identifier.
Environment suffix.
Azure location.
Search service SKU.
Enable public network access. Combine with allowedIps for the deployer-IP allowlist pattern.
IPv4 addresses / CIDR ranges allowed on the public endpoint.
Whether admin/query API keys can be used to authenticate. Defaults to false (Entra ID / RBAC only).
Subnet ID where the PE lives.
Private DNS zone IDs for the searchService PE.
Tags applied to the search service and PE.

resources.name: [variables('searchName')]
resources.properties: [union(createObject('replicaCount', 1, 'partitionCount', 1, 'hostingMode', 'default', 'publicNetworkAccess', if(parameters('publicNetworkAccessEnabled'), 'enabled', 'disabled'), 'disableLocalAuth', not(parameters('localAuthenticationEnabled')), 'networkRuleSet', createObject('bypass', 'AzureServices', 'ipRules', variables('searchIpRules'))), if(parameters('localAuthenticationEnabled'), createObject('authOptions', createObject('aadOrApiKey', createObject('aadAuthFailureMode', 'http401WithBearerChallenge'))), createObject()))]

metadata.description: Private endpoint name.
Azure location.
Resource ID of the target service (e.g. storage account, cosmos, cognitive account).
List of subresource / groupId names to connect (e.g. ["blob"], ["account"], ["Sql"]).
Resource ID of the subnet where the PE NIC lives.
List of private DNS zone resource IDs that the PE registers its A record(s) in.
Optional. Custom name for the DNS zone group. Defaults to `default`.
Tags applied to the PE.
Short project identifier.
Environment suffix.
Azure location.
Cognitive Services kind. Use "AIServices" for AI Foundry.
SKU name.
Custom subdomain / privatelink hostname prefix for the account. Required for Entra ID / MI auth and for a PE to be attachable.
Enable Foundry project management on the account.
Enable public network access. Combine with networkAclsDefaultAction=Deny + networkAclsIpRules for the deployer-IP allowlist pattern.
Default action for network ACLs on the public endpoint.
Bypass setting for network ACLs. Foundry needs AzureServices bypass so Microsoft-managed control-plane can reach the account.
IPv4 addresses allowed on the public endpoint. Bare IPs only -- Cognitive Services rejects /31 and /32 CIDR notation.
Enable local (key) auth. Defaults to false.
Subnet ID where the account PE lives.
Private DNS zone IDs for the "account" subresource PE (3 zones: cognitiveservices, openai, services.ai).
Optional subnet ID for Foundry Agent Service network injection. When set, agent-runtime compute is injected into this subnet. Must be delegated to Microsoft.App/environments.
Tags applied to the account and PE.

resources.name: [variables('accountName')]
resources.properties: [union(createObject('customSubDomainName', parameters('customSubdomainName'), 'allowProjectManagement', parameters('projectManagementEnabled'), 'disableLocalAuth', not(parameters('localAuthEnabled')), 'publicNetworkAccess', if(parameters('publicNetworkAccessEnabled'), 'Enabled', 'Disabled'), 'networkAcls', createObject('defaultAction', parameters('networkAclsDefaultAction'), 'bypass', parameters('networkAclsBypass'), 'ipRules', variables('cognitiveIpRules'), 'virtualNetworkRules', createArray())), if(empty(parameters('agentSubnetId')), createObject(), createObject('networkInjections', createArray(createObject('scenario', 'agent', 'subnetArmId', parameters('agentSubnetId'), 'useMicrosoftManagedNetwork', false())))))]

metadata.description: Private endpoint name.
Azure location.
Resource ID of the target service (e.g. storage account, cosmos, cognitive account).
List of subresource / groupId names to connect (e.g. ["blob"], ["account"], ["Sql"]).

Optional. Custom name for the DNS zone group. Defaults to `default`.

Environment suffix (e.g. "dev").
Azure location.
Optional override for the project name. Default: proj-<baseName>-<environment>-<location>.
Optional Foundry project display name. Default matches projectName.
Optional description shown in the Foundry portal.
Name of the parent Cognitive Services / AIServices account (created by cognitive_account module).
Name of the Storage account backing the project.
Primary blob endpoint of the storage account (e.g. https://<name>.blob.core.windows.net/).
Name of the Cosmos DB account backing the project.
Cosmos DB document endpoint (e.g. https://<name>.documents.azure.com:443/).
Name of the AI Search service backing the project.
AI Search endpoint (e.g. https://<name>.search.windows.net).
Create the account + project capability hosts (Foundry Agent Service Standard Setup). Set false to defer.
Tags applied to created resources.
